package rulecoverage

import (
	"context"
	"os"
)

// Positive scenario for constructor_reads_env_directly.
// Rule intent: Constructors that call os.Getenv instead of receiving normalized config.
type OrderService struct {
	dbURL    string
	redisURL string
	apiKey   string
}

// NewOrderService reads environment variables directly in the constructor
// instead of accepting a config struct or injected values.
func NewOrderService(ctx context.Context) *OrderService {
	dbURL := os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL")
	redisURL := os.Getenv("REDIS_URL")
	apiKey := os.Getenv("STRIPE_API_KEY")

	if dbURL == "" {
		dbURL = "postgres://localhost:5432/orders"
	}

	return &OrderService{
		dbURL:    dbURL,
		redisURL: redisURL,
		apiKey:   apiKey,
	}
}

// NewPaymentService demonstrates the same anti-pattern in a different constructor.
func NewPaymentService() *PaymentService {
	secretKey := os.Getenv("PAYMENT_SECRET_KEY")
	webhookURL := os.Getenv("PAYMENT_WEBHOOK_URL")

	return &PaymentService{
		secretKey:  secretKey,
		webhookURL: webhookURL,
	}
}

type PaymentService struct {
	secretKey  string
	webhookURL string
}
